content.principles: 1. Execution Stack: JS runs on a single thread. 2. Task Queues: Divided into Macrotasks (setTimeout, setInterval, I/O) and Microtasks (Promises, process.nextTick, MutationObserver). 3. The Loop: Execute stack -> Clear all Microtasks -> Render UI (if needed) -> Execute one Macrotask -> Repeat. Microtasks always have higher priority than the next Macrotask.
pitfalls: Blocking the main thread with heavy computation stops UI responsiveness.
Infinite microtask recursion (e.g., recursive Promise.resolve) starving the Macrotask queue/UI.
Misunderstanding the order of process.nextTick vs Promise (Node.js specific).
follow_up_paths: Browser vs Node.js Event Loop differences
Web Workers for offloading main thread
